steps.title: Business Registration
steps.desc: Register your business as a Sole Proprietorship, Partnership, or Private Limited Company. This makes your business legal and is the first step to exporting.

steps.title: Get a National Tax Number (NTN)
steps.desc: Apply for an NTN from the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) online using the IRIS portal. Your NTN is your main identity for all tax and export purposes.

steps.title: Open a Business Bank Account
steps.desc: Open a bank account in your exact business name. You will need this to receive foreign currency payments from your international buyers.

steps.title: Sales Tax Registration (STRN)
steps.desc: Register for Sales Tax with the FBR to get your STRN. This is important for tax refunds and is required for exporting many types of goods.

steps.title: Join a Chamber of Commerce
steps.desc: Become a member of your local Chamber of Commerce (like KCCI or LCCI) or a relevant trade association. You will need them to issue 'Certificates of Origin' later.

steps.title: Register on Pakistan Single Window (PSW)
steps.desc: Sign up on the PSW portal. This is a mandatory, all-in-one government system where you will file all your customs declarations and export documents online.

steps.title: Find International Buyers
steps.desc: Look for buyers using online platforms (like Alibaba), attending international trade shows, or getting help from the Trade Development Authority of Pakistan (TDAP).

steps.title: Send Product Samples
steps.desc: Send a small sample of your product to interested buyers via courier (like DHL or FedEx) so they can test the quality before making a large order.

steps.title: Negotiate the Deal & Incoterms
steps.desc: Agree on the product price, quantity, and 'Incoterms' (who pays for shipping). For example, 'FOB' means you pay to load it on the ship, 'CIF' means you also pay for freight and insurance.

steps.title: Issue a Proforma Invoice (PI)
steps.desc: Send a Proforma Invoice to the buyer. This is a preliminary bill that confirms all the details of the order. The buyer needs this to arrange payment.

steps.title: Sign the Sales Contract
steps.desc: Sign a formal, legal contract with your buyer. It should clearly state what you are selling, the price, the delivery time, and the payment method.

steps.title: Agree on Payment Method
steps.desc: Decide how you will be paid. The safest methods are 'Advance Payment' (getting paid before shipping) or 'Letter of Credit (LC)' (a bank guarantee).

steps.title: Receive Advance or Letter of Credit
steps.desc: Wait until your bank confirms they have received the Advance Payment or the Letter of Credit from the buyer's bank. Do not ship without this!

steps.title: Submit Form-E via Bank
steps.desc: Apply for a 'Form-E' through your bank. This is an electronic form that tells the State Bank of Pakistan you are exporting goods and bringing foreign money into the country.

steps.title: Bank Approves Form-E
steps.desc: Once your bank verifies the payment or LC, they will approve your Form-E. You cannot export anything without an approved Form-E.

steps.title: Manufacture & Pack the Goods
steps.desc: Produce or source the goods and pack them securely for international travel. Make sure to label the boxes correctly as required by the destination country.

steps.title: Book a Freight Forwarder
steps.desc: Hire a freight forwarder or clearing agent. They will book space for your goods on a ship or airplane and handle the complex logistics.

steps.title: Prepare the Packing List
steps.desc: Create a detailed Packing List. This document tells customs and the buyer exactly how many boxes there are, what is inside each box, and their weights.

steps.title: Issue the Commercial Invoice
steps.desc: Create the final Commercial Invoice. This is the official bill of sale that must match your Proforma Invoice exactly.

steps.title: Get Special Certificates
steps.desc: If you are exporting food or plants, get a Phytosanitary Certificate. You might also need a Certificate of Origin from your Chamber of Commerce.

steps.title: File Goods Declaration (GD)
steps.desc: Your customs agent will submit a Goods Declaration (GD) online through the PSW portal. This tells Pakistan Customs exactly what is leaving the country.

steps.title: Customs Inspection & Loading
steps.desc: Your goods will arrive at the port. Customs and the Anti-Narcotics Force (ANF) may inspect them. Once cleared, the goods are loaded onto the ship or plane.

steps.title: Get the Bill of Lading (B/L)
steps.desc: Once the ship departs, the shipping company will give you a Bill of Lading (or Airway Bill for flights). This is the most important document—it proves the goods were shipped.

steps.title: Send Documents to the Buyer
steps.desc: Submit the Bill of Lading, Commercial Invoice, Packing List, and Form-E to your bank. Your bank will send them to the buyer's bank so the buyer can claim the goods.

steps.title: Receive Final Payment & Close Form-E
steps.desc: Once the buyer receives the documents and pays any remaining balance, the money comes to your bank. Your bank will then 'Close' the Form-E, and your export is officially complete!

clauses.title: SUBJECT MATTER
clauses.text: The Seller agrees to sell, and the Buyer agrees to purchase, the following goods (the "Goods"): {description}, in the quantity, quality, and specification mutually agreed between the Parties.

clauses.title: CONTRACT VALUE
clauses.text: The total contract value payable by the Buyer to the Seller for the Goods under this Contract is PKR {contractValue}, inclusive of all charges expressly agreed between the Parties, exclusive of any charges arising after risk transfer under Clause 3.

clauses.title: INCOTERMS
clauses.text: Delivery of the Goods shall be governed by the Incoterms rule {incoterms} (as published by the International Chamber of Commerce, current revision), which shall determine the point of transfer of risk and cost between the Seller and the Buyer.

clauses.title: DELIVERY DATE
clauses.text: The Seller shall ship or otherwise make available the Goods on or before {deliveryDate}, subject to extension for causes beyond the Seller's reasonable control, including force majeure events.

clauses.title: PAYMENT
clauses.text: The Buyer shall remit payment of the contract value to the Seller in accordance with the payment terms and banking instructions communicated separately by the Seller, prior to or against shipping documents, as agreed between the Parties.

clauses.title: INSPECTION & ACCEPTANCE
clauses.text: The Buyer shall have the right to inspect the Goods prior to shipment. Acceptance of the Goods shall be deemed final upon delivery in accordance with the agreed Incoterms, absent written notice of non-conformity within a reasonable period thereafter.

clauses.title: FORCE MAJEURE
clauses.text: Neither Party shall be liable for delay or failure in performance resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, war, strikes, or governmental restrictions.

clauses.title: GOVERNING LAW & JURISDICTION
clauses.text: This Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of {governingLaw}, and any disputes arising hereunder shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts thereof.

clauses.title: ENTIRE AGREEMENT
clauses.text: This Contract constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, or agreements, whether written or oral.

proformaInvoice.declaration: We declare that this invoice shows the actual price of the goods described and that all particulars are true and correct. hiihi
